api_kwargs.system:
You are extracting menu items from a restaurant menu image for POS import.
Read the image carefully — it is the primary source of truth.
OCR text is provided as a hint but often contains errors — always trust the image.

Extract every orderable item from this restaurant menu for POS system import.

Each item = one product a customer can order in a POS system.
Split compound items: "Beef or Chicken Empanadas" → two separate items.
Each named topping (Pepperoni, Mushrooms, etc.) and each named sauce (Ranch, BBQ, etc.) is a separate item — do not combine them into one "Each Topping Add" item.
Section-wide choices (e.g., "Naked or Breaded", "White or Wheat") → size variants on each item, not descriptions.
Section-wide notes (e.g., "All sandwiches come with lettuce, tomato...") → include in each item's description.
When a section has multiple price columns (e.g., "Regular/Deluxe", "W/Fries", "W/Cheese"), capture each column as a size variant.
Use Title Case for names even if the menu is printed in ALL CAPS.

IMPORTANT: Match each description to its correct item. Menu descriptions often appear on the line below the item name — verify the pairing is right. If uncertain, set description to null.
Do not skip items — check every section of the menu for completeness.

For each item return:
- "name": exact full name as printed on the menu
- "description": menu description if shown, null otherwise
- "price": price as float, 0 if not visible
- "category": one of: Pizza, Toppings, Appetizers, Salads, Soups, Sandwiches, Burgers, Wraps, Entrees, Seafood, Pasta, Steaks, Wings, Sauces, Sides, Desserts, Beverages, Kids Menu, Breakfast, Calzones, Subs, Platters, Other
- "sizes": [{"label": "...", "price": N.NN}] for items with multiple price points

Output ONLY valid JSON: {"items": [...]}

response.thinking_text:
Let me carefully analyze the menu image and extract all items. I'll go through each section systematically.

Starting from the top left:

**PIZZA**

First, the pizza sizes and pricing structure. I see columns for 10" Med, 12" Sm(?), 14"(?) and Family Size.

Let me re-read. The columns appear to be: 10" Med, 12" Set(?), 14"(?), Family Size.

Actually looking more carefully: 10" Med, 12" Set, 14"(Lg?), Family Size (17x25?)

**PIZZA & CALZONE TOPPINGS**
- CHEESE: 8.00, 11.50, 13.95, 22.50


- EACH TOPPING ADD: 1.50, 2.25, 2.75, 4.00

Now I'm cataloging the available toppings across meat and vegetable options, plus the sauce selections. The meat choices include pepperoni, sausage, hamburger, chicken, bacon, and ham. For vegetables, there's a longer list spanning mushrooms, fresh tomatoes, pineapple, eggplant, peppers, garlic, olives, broccoli, spinach, onions, hot peppers, banana peppers, and roasted red peppers. Customers can also choose from red, white, pesto, garlic, alfredo, or ranch sauce.

Looking at the gourmet pizza menu, I'm seeing four size options with corresponding prices. The 4 Cheese combines ricotta, mozzarella, and provolone. Meat Lovers stacks pepperoni, sausage, bacon, ham, and hamburger. The Combination brings together pepperoni, hamburger, sausage with onions, peppers, and mushrooms. Mexican features hamburger, onions, hot peppers, and cheddar. There are also BBQ, Blue Cheese, and Honey Mustard sauce options available.

Continuing through the specialty pizzas, Hawaiian pairs bacon, pineapple, and ham with a choice of red or white sauce. The Gyro Special uses gyro meat, tomatoes, onions, and feta cheese. Philly Steak combines steak with sautéed onions, peppers, and mushrooms. Pesto Chicken features grilled chicken with pesto sauce and tomato. Grilled Chicken Bacon Ranch rounds out the selection. Most of these specialty pizzas are priced consistently across the three sizes at 17.95, 25.50, and 34.75.

Now I'm looking at more specialty options like Buffalo Chicken Bacon Ranch with heat level choices, Buffalo Chicken with similar options, Pollo Chicken with broccoli and alfredo, a Veggie option loaded with peppers, mushrooms, onions, broccoli, tomato, and garlic, and Mediterranean with onion, peppers, eggplant, spinach, tomatoes, olives, feta, and red onion. These follow the same pricing structure.

Continuing through the menu, there's a Turkish option, Lasagna Pizza, Margarita with fresh basil and mozzarella, what looks like a Margherita Fan Pizza variant, Beef Taco Pizza with seasoned ground beef, diced onions and peppers, cheddar and mozzarella, and Potato Bacon Pizza featuring sliced potatoes, bacon, and a blend of cheddar and mozzarella cheeses. All maintain the consistent three-size pricing at 17.95, 25.50, and 34.75. Now I'm looking at the calzone menu options, which are all stuffed with ricotta and mozzarella and served with sauce on the side, ranging from a basic cheese option up through various protein and topping combinations like steak, buffalo chicken, and chicken pesto, with prices varying by size. Looking at the appetizers section, I'm seeing various fried items and sides like chicken tenders, fried chicken with fries, onion rings, and curly fries, along with some newer nacho options that come with different proteins and toppings.

Now I'm moving through the rest of the appetizer menu—mozzarella sticks, garlic bread, salads, and some Mediterranean options like stuffed grape leaves and spinach pie with tzatziki. There's also a sampler platter combining several fried items. I'm noticing the soups are priced at $6.50, and I need to check the pricing structure more carefully since there appear to be two price columns in the image.

Looking at the buffalo wings section now—they come naked or breaded with blue cheese on the side, and the pricing scales up significantly with quantity: 6 pieces at $9.95, 10 pieces at $15, 20 pieces, and 50 pieces options, with fries available as an add-on. There are also boneless wings and buffalo chicken tenders at various portion sizes, and they offer multiple sauce flavors including hot, mild, BBQ, and honey mustard.

Now I'm seeing the burger section with additional sauce options like garlic romano, Cajun, teriyaki, and Jack Daniels BBQ. The 6 oz Angus burgers are build-your-own style with regular and deluxe pricing tiers—basic burger at $9 or $13, bacon and cheeseburger variations around $9.99 to $13.99, double burgers up to $15.99, and specialty options like the Russian burger with mushrooms, BBQ sauce, cheddar, and bacon at $11.99 or $15.99, plus a 1000 Island burger with mushrooms, pickles, lettuce, and tomato.

Moving into club sandwiches now—all of them come with lettuce, tomato, bacon, fries, and pickles for $14 each, with options ranging from classic BLT and turkey to roast beef, chicken cutlet, grilled chicken, and steak and cheese, plus a Manhattan club combining turkey and ham. Then there are melt sandwiches where you can add bacon for an extra dollar. Looking at the wrap options, continuing through the gyro varieties, tuna, chicken ranch combinations, and specialty items like buffalo chicken and Russian chicken cutlet wraps. Looking at the rest of the menu items, I'm seeing more wrap options like the Mexican with grilled chicken and avocado, along with steak-based wraps and a taco wrap with ground beef. Now I'm trying to parse the pricing information, though the numbers are a bit unclear in what I'm looking at.

Let me organize this more systematically by going through each section carefully. For the pizza toppings, I need to break these out individually—there are meat options like pepperoni, sausage, and chicken, plus a whole range of vegetables from mushrooms to roasted red peppers. I'm also noting the sauce choices available: red, white, pesto, and several creamy options like alfredo and ranch.

Now I'm working through the appetizers section, which has some pricing I need to verify. The garlic knots are clearly marked at $6.95, and the nacho variations are around $12.95 each. But I'm seeing several fried items and sides where the prices aren't fully visible or legible in the image—things like french fries, onion rings, chicken tenders with various combinations, mozzarella sticks, and garlic bread. I need to be careful about which items have prices I can actually read versus which ones are unclear.

Looking at the rest of the appetizer list, there are more items like salads, stuffed grape leaves, potato skins with different toppings, jalapeño poppers, a sampler, and spinach pie. I'm trying to cross-reference what I can see in the image with the OCR data to get accurate pricing, and it looks like there might be two price columns—possibly regular and a cheese or upgraded version. Let me focus on the items where I can clearly make out the prices first. I'm noticing the appetizers have some missing prices, and the wraps appear to be $14.00. For wings, I can see the pricing structure with options for different quantities and whether they come with fries, though a few prices are cut off. Let me compile all this into a comprehensive JSON extraction with the visible pricing data. I'm realizing the OCR is conflating different menu sections, so let me focus on what I can actually make out from the image itself rather than relying on the text parsing. I can see some pricing information for wraps toward the bottom of the menu. Now I'm going back through the appetizers section to get accurate pricing. I can see items like chicken bacon nachos, french fries with a cheese option, curly fries, onion rings, and chicken tenders, each with their respective prices. Let me pull together the complete menu data with all the prices I can make out clearly. Looking at the gourmet pizza section, I'm seeing three price tiers across the sizes—around 14.75 for the smallest, then 17.95, 25.50, and 34.75 for the larger options. For the wraps, there are base prices around 11.95 and separate pricing when ordered with fries, so I need to capture both columns to get the complete picture.
